nodes.name: Telegram Trigger
nodes.notes:
Listens for all new messages in the Telegram signals chat.
The bot must be added to the group/channel as an admin with privacy mode disabled so it can see all messages (not just /commands).
Only triggers on 'message' events — ignores edits, reactions, etc.

nodes.name: Filter Trading Signals
nodes.notes:
Filters incoming messages to only pass through those that look like trading signals.
Matches (case-insensitive): BUY, SELL, LONG, SHORT, ENTRY, SL, TP, ALERT, or ticker symbols like $AAPL.
Messages that don't match any of these are silently dropped — no error, no downstream processing.
Uses OR logic: any single keyword match is enough to pass.

nodes.name: Format Signal Prompt
nodes.notes:
Extracts the message text from the Telegram payload and builds the full analysis prompt.
Handles both group messages (message.text) and channel posts (channel_post.text).
Includes the fixed portfolio context: 11 positions, ~$56K exposure, AI/tech bias.
Also extracts metadata (chat title, date, message ID) for the log.

options.systemMessage:
You are a professional trading analyst assistant. You analyze incoming trading signals against an existing portfolio. Be direct, concise, and actionable. Always structure your response as:

1. SIGNAL SUMMARY: (ticker, direction, key levels)
2. FIT ANALYSIS: (how it fits with current AI/tech-heavy portfolio)
3. CORRELATION RISK: (overlap with existing 11 positions)
4. RISK LEVEL: (Low / Medium / High)
5. RECOMMENDATION: (TAKE IT / SKIP / WATCH — with brief reasoning)

Keep total response under 200 words.

nodes.name: Grok Signal Analyzer
nodes.notes:
AI Agent node that sends the formatted signal prompt to Grok for analysis.
Uses a structured system message to ensure consistent, actionable output.
Set to 'Continue On Fail' so a Grok API error won't crash the workflow.
The prompt includes the fixed portfolio context (11 positions, $56K, AI/tech bias).

nodes.name: xAI Grok Chat Model
nodes.notes:
xAI Grok Chat Model sub-node — connected to the AI Agent as its language model.
Model: grok-3-fast-beta (fast responses, good for real-time signal analysis).
Temperature: 0.3 (low — we want consistent, factual analysis, not creative output).
Max tokens: 1024 (plenty for a concise analysis).
You can change the model to grok-3-beta or grok-2-latest depending on your xAI plan.

nodes.name: Log Analysis
nodes.notes:
Structures the final output as a clean log entry: timestamp, original signal, Grok analysis, metadata.
Outputs to the execution log (visible in n8n execution history).
You can optionally connect this to a Google Sheets node, webhook, or database to persist the logs.
Set to 'Continue On Fail' for resilience.

nodes.name: No Operation (End)
nodes.notes:
Terminal node — marks the end of the workflow.
The analysis is logged in the execution history.
To extend: replace this with a Google Sheets, Slack, Discord, or Email node to forward the analysis.
